sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed with UP action
t = transitions[-1]
before_nodes = {n['color']: (n['hash'], n['pixels'], [p for p in n['boundary'][:4]]) for n in t.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['color']: (n['hash'], n['pixels'], [p for p in n['boundary'][:4]]) for n in t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
print("Before UP:", before_nodes)
print("After UP:", after_nodes)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! The blue bar moved UP:
- Before: rows 48-51
- After: rows 44-47
So UP moves the blue bar up! The blue bar is the player character. The W (white) and N (green) bars at the bottom also changed slightly - those are the timer/progress bars.
So the controls are:
- LEFT/RIGHT: move blue bar horizontally
- UP/DOWN: move blue bar vertically
- SPACE: unknown (maybe shoot/activate?)
Now I need to figure out the goal. The targets seem to be:
- Yellow blocks at top (rows 4-11, cols 44-55 and cols 56-63)
- Red block at rows 24-27, cols 28-39
- Magenta block at rows 56-59, cols 40-43
- Dark gray block at rows 60-62, cols 40-43
The goal is probably to "collect" or "destroy" the red and yellow blocks by moving the blue bar to them and pressing SPACE. Let me test this by moving the blue bar to the red block and pressing SPACE.
The red block is at rows 24-27, cols 28-39. The blue bar is currently at rows 44-47, cols 28-47. I need to move it up to rows 24-27.
Let me try moving UP several times to reach the red block's row, then press SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move blue bar up to the red block's row and press SPACE
# Blue bar is at rows 44-47, red block at rows 24-27
# Need to move up about 5 times (each UP moves 4 rows)
action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
